reviewer_objection: Why is shard 008 framed as a source-depth demonstration instead of as a procedural-contamination or research-misconduct count surface?
bounded_response_now: Because the package is intentionally heterogeneous and still thin at the top authority classes: Panel A has one document-grade Italian regulator-order anchor plus one Finnish direct-source-resolved but non-official comparator, while Panel B spans mediated editor statement, blocked DOI-pair metadata, two direct Springer-originating notice-text exemplars across journal and book surfaces, and a mixed residual bucket containing one archived SSRN platform-action exemplar plus two still-weak residual rows. That structure supports evidence-depth narration and claim ceilings, not flat counts, prevalence, sanctions, or misconduct tallies.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Yes: Table 1E stays a bounded evidence-depth panel rather than an incident inventory.
visualization_or_dashboard_hook: Table 1E reviewer-warning note ; evidence-tier funnel ; thesis claim-ceiling map.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Do not widen rhetoric until one publisher-diverse direct notice-text exemplar and one second institution-confirmed or document-grade procedural family land.

reviewer_objection: Does the Finland Yle row already give LIMEN a second authoritative procedural-contamination family?
bounded_response_now: No. The Finland row is reviewer-legible and valuable precisely because it is direct-source resolved and paired with bounded official-probe metadata, but it remains below official phenomenon confirmation. It diversifies jurisdiction and language coverage, not the document-grade authority ceiling.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Yes: multilingual comparator status remains visible while official confirmation stays explicitly absent.
visualization_or_dashboard_hook: Authority-escalation ladder ; jurisdiction-language caution overlay ; legal-uncertainty matrix.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Upgrade only if one Finnish police, court, ministry, or judicial-process document independently confirms the phenomenon or procedural response.

reviewer_objection: Why are the SAGE and NEJM rows not treated as direct-notice research-integrity exemplars if article identity and retraction metadata are already visible?
bounded_response_now: Because direct-notice grade requires publisher- or journal-originating notice text, not only a named editor quote or a retraction DOI pair. The SAGE row is still a mediated editor statement with verified article identity, and the NEJM row is still a blocked-notice DOI-pair state under publisher access friction.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Yes: direct-notice status remains exclusive to the captured Springer notice text.
visualization_or_dashboard_hook: Blocked-notice facet ; notice-depth funnel ; source-friction panel.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Capture one direct SAGE or NEJM notice text or an equivalent public publisher-originating record before promoting either row.

reviewer_objection: If LIMEN already has two direct Springer-originating notices, why is the research-integrity slice still considered thin?
bounded_response_now: Because two same-family direct notices do not by themselves support cross-publisher or cross-venue generalization. The current slice has a two-row top-depth direct-notice pair, but both rows remain inside the Springer family; below them the package still includes mediated and blocked-notice intermediate rows plus a mixed residual bucket containing one archived SSRN platform-action exemplar and two weaker unresolved rows. The publication value is the modeled source-depth ladder, not a mature corpus of settled publisher actions.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Yes: the Springer pair now anchors the top depth while cross-publisher generalization stays blocked.
visualization_or_dashboard_hook: Research-integrity source-depth ladder ; top-depth blocker badge ; subtype-by-source-family heatmap.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Prioritize one publisher-diverse direct journal/publisher notice-text exemplar from NEJM, SAGE, or another compliant public notice route before more packaging work.

reviewer_objection: Why keep the weak residual bucket visible instead of dropping it from Table 1E?
bounded_response_now: Because visible quarantine is scientifically cleaner than silent exclusion. The residual bucket records that some leads are still supported only by specialist reporting or platform-gated discovery surfaces, which is itself a methods-relevant negative result about source-resolution friction. Removing the bucket would falsely imply that the package is cleaner and more resolved than it is.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Yes: weak-source rows remain quarantined rather than mixed into stronger authority classes.
visualization_or_dashboard_hook: Residual-category panel ; blocked-source queue ; methods/limitations appendix note.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Escape one residual lead only with a direct platform, publisher, indexer, or institutional action record; otherwise keep the quarantine visible.

reviewer_objection: Is shard 008 publishable even though it still has only one document-grade procedural anchor and only a Springer-concentrated direct-notice pair on the research-integrity side?
bounded_response_now: Yes, but as an evidence-architecture and hostile-reviewer methods contribution rather than as a denominator-heavy empirical package. The publishable unit is a traceable two-branch ladder showing why procedural-contamination and research-integrity rows require different source-depth handling, explicit caption controls, upgrade triggers, and forbidden overreads.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Yes: article-ready value remains methodological, interpretive, and dashboard-safe rather than prevalence-oriented.
visualization_or_dashboard_hook: Reviewer-response appendix ; Table 1E legend bundle ; thesis evidence-architecture section.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Reuse this objection ledger in manuscript/dashboard/thesis assembly and spend the next substantive cycle on one threshold-changing authority upgrade rather than more packaging drift repair.
